data.title: Intellia to cut 27% of workforce, discontinue NTLA-3001
data.description:
Intellia Therapeutics (NASDAQ:NTLA) said it plans to focus its resources on developing drug candidates NTLA-2002 and nex-z and discontinue development of NTLA-3001, which will result in the company cutting around 27% of its workforce.

The biotech company said it sees NTLA-2002 and nex-z as having "the greatest opportunity to create significant, near-term value.” NTLA-2002 is being developed for hereditary angioedema and nex-z for ATTR amyloidosis.

NTLA-3001 was being developed for the treatment of alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency-associated lung disease. The company is also discontinuing certain research-stage programs.

Intellia (NASDAQ:NTLA) said it expects to incur charges of around $8M in Q1 2025 related to the restructuring. The company ended 2024 with approximately $862M in cash, equivalents and investments, which should be able to fund operations into the first half of 2027, according to a statement.

The company also announced that its chief scientific officer, Laura Sepp-Lorenzino, was retiring. She will continue with the company as a senior scientific advisor through the remainder of the year. Intellia's (NASDAQ:NTLA) head of immunology and cell therapy, Birgit Schultes, will assume the post of CSO on Jan. 13.

Intellia (NTLA) added that it plans to complete the buildout of its commercial leadership team by the second half of 2025.

data.title: Eni poised for €3B in asset sales this year, UBS says in downgrade
data.description:
Eni's (NYSE:E) asset selloff plan is more than halfway completed and is far ahead of schedule, UBS analysts said Thursday, adding that the remaining disposals this year likely will be focused primarily on the upstream business.

Eni's (NYSE:E) strategic plan calls for selling ~€8B in assets through 2027, with nearly half coming from sales of stakes in company units and the other half from upstream activities, and UBS analysts led by Joshua Stone said the company already has sold assets totaling €5.9B (~$6.1B), while another €3B of sales should be announced by the end of 2025.

But UBS downgraded Eni (E) shares to Neutral from Buy with a €14 price target, down from the previous €15.50 PT, saying the company's rapid advancements have led to a situation where there is "less room for positive surprise" in earnings and the scope of buybacks for 2025.

"We see a risk that Eni becomes a victim of its own success," the analysts wrote. "Given the fast progress made, we think most of the good news is priced in, and the risk is slightly skewed to the downside should there be any delays."

The bank also noted Eni (E) shares currently trade at a 5% premium to their historical enterprise value to debt-adjusted cash flow ratio, suggesting limited potential for further appreciation.

data.title: Brookdale Senior Living occupancy increases to 79.3% in December
data.description:
• Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) on Thursday reported that its weighted average occupancy increased 100 basis points year-over-year, from 78.3% in 2023 to 79.3% in December 2024.
  • Fourth quarter 2024 weighted average occupancy also increased 100 basis points compared to the prior year quarter.
  • Fourth quarter 2024 move-in volume surpassed the prior year and exceeded the pre-pandemic average for this period by 8%.
  • BKD -2.86% after hours to $4.74.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ImmuCell reports higher prelim Q4 sales
data.description:
• ImmuCell Corporation (NASDAQ:ICCC) announced preliminary sales of $7.8M in Q4, a 52% increase from Q4 2023.
  • Co sees FY 2024 sales of $26.5M, also a 52% increase from 2023.
  • The company believes that the current backlog reflects strong demand as distributors work to replenish their buffer stocks and as end-users place orders in advance of peak calving season during the first quarter.

  • Source: ImmuCell Corporation

data.title: Rocket Lab selected by NASA to provide Neutron launch services under VADR contract
data.description:
• Rocket Lab (NASDAQ:RKLB) said on Thursday that it has reached an agreement with NASA to include Neutron launch services to the agency through Rocket Lab’s existing VADR (Venture-Class Acquisition of Dedicated and Rideshare) contract.
  • Rocket Lab’s new medium-lift reusable rocket Neutron allows the opportunity for Rocket Lab to continue broadening access to space to deliver multiple missions across a range of orbits, including CubeSats, Class D missions, and other payloads.
  • Neutron is also ideally placed to be on-ramped on to the U.S. Government’s National Security Space Launch (NSSL) Lane 1 program, an IDIQ contract valued at $5.6 billion over a five-year period, Rocket Lab (RKLB) said.
  • Neutron is scheduled for its debut launch from Rocket Lab Launch Complex 3 in Virginia from mid-2025.

data.title: BlackRock bows out of climate group amid antitrust concerns
data.description:
BlackRock (NYSE:BLK), the U.S.'s largest asset manager, has decided to exit a major climate group, succumbing to pressure from Republican politicians who have criticized the firm's commitment to addressing global warming, according to a media report dated Thursday.

Stoking the departure, BlackRock's (NYSE:BLK) memberships in certain climate groups, including the Net Zero Asset Managers initiative (NZAMi), "have caused confusion regarding BlackRock’s practices and subjected us to legal inquiries from various public officials," the firm said in a letter to clients, Bloomberg reported.

BlackRock (BLK) has faced criticism from Republican lawmakers for adopting policies labeled as "woke" by conservatives. In November, it was sued by a group of states led by Texas, accusing the BLK of boosting electricity prices through their investments, marking the highest-profile suit against the ESG industry.

“Our participation in NZAMi didn’t impact the way we managed client portfolios,” BlackRock said in the letter. “Therefore, our departure doesn’t change the way we develop products and solutions for clients or how we manage their portfolios.”

As recently as September, BlackRock (BLK) managed over $1T in sustainable and transition investment strategies, it noted, Bloomberg reported. Overseeing about $50T, NZAMi manages some 325 asset managers seeking to achieve net zero alignment by 2050.

BlackRock's (BLK) exit comes in the wake of an extensive outflow of U.S. members from the Net-Zero Banking Alliance (NZBA) -- which reflects Wall Street's wish to protect itself from the growing political pressure with President-elect Donald Trump's return to the White House. Over the past month, the NZBA lost Goldman Sachs (GS), Wells Fargo (WFC), Bank of America (BAC), Citigroup (C), JPMorgan Chase (JPM) and Morgan Stanley (MS).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Prospera Energy's 1.2M securities acquired by Vittorio Valsecchi
data.description:
• On January 7, 2025, Vittorio Valsecchi, through Valsecchi Immobilien AG, a company under Valsecchi's control and direction, acquired 1.2M shares of Prospera Energy (TSXV:PEI:CA); at ~C$0.035 per share for an aggregate purchase price of C$42,489.42.  

  • Prior to the acquisition, Valsecchi beneficially owned an aggregate of 53.55M shares of Prospera, representing ~12.54% shares on a non-diluted basis. 

  • Valsecchi now, directly or indirectly, beneficially owns 60.75M shares, representing ~14.22% of the company on a non-diluted basis. 

  • Valsecchi acquired the shares for investment purposes. 

  • Source: Vittorio Valsecchi

data.title: Block changes ticker symbol to XYZ
data.description:
• Block (NYSE:SQ) said on Thursday that it will be changing its ticker symbols from “SQ” and “SQ2” to “XYZ”.
  • This is in connection with the company’s earlier name change to “Block” and its ecosystem of brands including Cash App, Square, Afterpay and TIDAL.
  • The change will be implemented across the company’s dual listings on the New York Stock Exchange and the Australian Securities Exchange.
  • Effective January 21, 2025, the company’s Class A common stock will trade on the NYSE under the new symbol “XYZ”. Effective January 22, 2025, the company’s ticker code on the ASX will also change to “XYZ”.

data.title: Block to change ticker symbol to 'XYZ'
data.description:
Block (NYSE:SQ) will change its ticker symbol to "XYZ" from "SQ" and SQ2", reflecting its broader range of businesses beyond its original Square payment system, the company said on Thursday.

The change will become effective on Jan. 21, 2025, for shares trading on the New York Stock Exchange. The change will take effect on Jan. 22 for shares traded on the Australian Securities Exchange.

The company now includes Cash App, Afterpay, and TIDAL, in addition to its Square seller system.

Block (NYSE:SQ) will release Q4 financial results on Feb. 20, 2025.

data.title: Sweden's army plans to buy $2B in tanks from KNDS
data.description:
• The Swedish army plans to buy tanks from German-French defense contractor KNDS for 22 billion Swedish crowns ($1.97 billion), Sweden's government said in a statement Thursday.
  • The order includes 44 Leopard 2 A8 tanks and upgrades for 66 existing tanks.
  • "This is an absolutely necessary investment," Swedish Defense Minister Pal Johnson said to public broadcaster SVT. The tanks will help the country to meet its goal of doubling the size of its army, he said.
  • Deliveries of the tanks will start in 2028 and run through 2031, according to the statement.
